OPPOSITION TO MTN FOR JUDGMNT ON THE PLEADINGS FILED BY PLAINTIFF BELLONI, JENNIFER
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-30-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS THE SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT AND THE CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF'S CONSTITUTIONAL CHALLENGE TO THE DEFENDANT'S PROCEDURES SATISFIES THE REQUIREMENT FOR WASTE. HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLED FACTS SUFFICIENT FOR AN INFERENCE THAT SHE HAS PAID AN ASSESSED TAX WITHIN THE MEANING OF CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 526A. SEE CORNELIUS V. LOS ANGELES COUNTY METROPOLITAN TRANSPORTATION AUTHORITY (1996) 49 CAL. APP. 4TH 1761, 1775-78. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND TO CURE THIS DEFECT, INCLUDING ADDING A PARTY THAT MEETS THE STANDING REQUIREMENTS IF NECESSARY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185
1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF BELLONI, JENNIFER AS TO DEFENDANT THE SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT DOES 1 TO 10 THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF BELLONI, JENNIFER

LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS SFPD & CCSF'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. EVEN IF THE COURT ASSUMES THAT THE AMENDED COMPLAINT DOES NOT TECHNICALLY COMPLY WITH THE ORDER OF MARCH 4, 2013, THERE IS LITTLE POINT IN GRANTING THIS MOTION. THE COURT WOULD GRANT THE MOTION WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. DEFENDANT'S REMEDY IS TO BRING A MOTION ATTACKING THE MERITS OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

Defendants Sfpd & Ccsf'S Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-20-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendants SFPD & CCSF'S Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint is denied. Even if the Court assumes that the amended complaint does not technically comply with the order of March 4, 2013, there is little point in granting this motion. The Court would grant the motion with leave to amend. Defendant's remedy is to bring a motion attacking the merits of the amended complaint. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-21-2013 Continued To Jun-19-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS THE SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT and THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to June 19, 2013 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302 to give Plaintiff the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Plaintiff must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than May 21, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be granted. The court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. Judge: Richard Kramer; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-03-2013 Continued To Jun-19-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgmenet On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Mar-04-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. TARA M. STEELEY, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-4655 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS THE SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT AND THE CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
is Granted with leave to amend. Plaintiff's constitutional challenge to the defendant's procedures satisfies the requirement for waste. However, Plaintiff has not pled facts sufficient for an inference that she has paid an assessed tax within the meaning of Code of Civil Procedure 526a. See Cornelius v. Los Angeles County Metropolitan Transportation Authority (1996) 49 Cal. App. 4th 1761, 1775-78. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to cure this defect, including adding a party that meets the standing requirements if necessary. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: A. James Robertson II, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/AJR
